title: my solution to autostart a specific connection then close mRemoteNG when this connection is closed
author: petit982
body_snippet:
Hi all,
I'm using mRemoteNG since YESTERDAY because I had issues with Windows standard RDP connection (freeze every 1 min).
With mRemoteNG, same server, same user, same PC >>> ZERO PROBLEMS... PERFECT...

But I noticed mRemoteNG had a few limitations:
- no way to open a specific connection at startup
- no way to auto-close mRemoteNG after this connection have been closed


So I wrote a BAT script to make the following: 
-1- search the string Connected="false" in the config file ...\App...

iterations.description: Implement new feature to allow autostarting a specified connection via command-line argument and closing the application when that connection terminates.
Verified: feature already implemented in codebase

comments.author: AlexandreBourrieau
comments.snippet: Thanks you you save my life !

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): Implement new feature to allow autostarting a specified connection via command-line argument and closing the application when that connection terminates.
Approach: Introduce a new command-line argument (e.g., --autostart-connection) to specify a connection by name. Implement logic in StartupArgumentsInterpreter and FrmMain to parse this argument, initiate the specified connection, and subscribe to the ConnectionInitiator.ConnectionClosed event. Upon closure of the autostarted connection, and if no other active connections exist, trigger Application.Exit(). Add a new option to control if other connections should prevent the application from closing.
